_meta.author: Employee 002 (CLM Backend)
_meta.purpose: Detection rules for auto-generating extraction_contract from raw site evidence. Each rule is directly implementable — detection method uses specific HTML selectors, JSON paths, or regex.
_meta.coverage: 6 evidence patterns covering ~70% of e-commerce product listing pages

step2_json_parse.description: For each tile, html_lib.unescape(tile.get('data-gtm')) then json.loads
step2_json_parse.pass_condition: At least 1 tile's data-gtm parses without JSONDecodeError

step3_ecommerce_items.description: Check _dig(payload, 'ecommerce', 'items') returns non-empty list with first element being a dict containing 'item_name' or 'item_id'
step3_ecommerce_items.pass_condition: len(ecommerce_items) > 0 and isinstance(ecommerce_items[0], dict) and ('item_name' in ecommerce_items[0] or 'item_id' in ecommerce_items[0])

gtm_data_attribute_extractor.required_evidence: Raw HTML string from requests/curl_cffi/browser mode containing at least one .product-tile[data-gtm] with parseable ecommerce.items
gtm_data_attribute_extractor.optional_evidence: Sibling HTML elements: img.tile-image[srcset] for image_url, .tile-image-wrapper-link[href] for product_url
failure_conditions: HTML has no elements matching '.product-tile[data-gtm]' AND no data-gtm regex matches in text nodes
All data-gtm attributes fail JSON parse after html_lib.unescape
Parsed JSON has no 'ecommerce' key or 'items' array is empty
ecommerce.items[0] is not a dict or lacks 'item_name'/'item_id'
gtm_data_attribute_extractor.why_low_false_positive: data-gtm attribute with ecommerce.items containing item_name/item_id is a GTM-specific tracking structure. Only sites instrumented with Google Tag Manager e-commerce tracking produce this. Non-product pages never have .product-tile elements. Blog, home, and article pages have zero .product-tile[data-gtm] elements.

step1_coerce_json.description: If evidence is str, try extract_next_data_json_from_html(evidence) to parse __NEXT_DATA__. If result is dict/list, continue.
step1_coerce_json.code: data = _coerce_json(evidence); if not isinstance(data, (dict, list)): return None

step2_find_wall.description: Navigate to data.props.pageProps.initialState.Wall OR data.Wall. Check for 'productGroupings' key.
step2_find_wall.pass_condition: isinstance(wall, dict) and isinstance(wall.get('productGroupings'), list) and len(wall['productGroupings']) > 0

step3_nike_shape.description: Check first product has Nike-specific nested shape: product.get('copy', {}).get('title') and product.get('prices', {}).get('currentPrice')
step3_nike_shape.code: product = groupings[0]['products'][0]; copy = product.get('copy', {}); prices = product.get('prices', {})
step3_nike_shape.pass_condition: isinstance(copy.get('title'), str) and copy['title'] != '' and isinstance(prices.get('currentPrice'), (int, float))

next_data_product_wall_extractor.required_evidence: Pre-parsed product list from __NEXT_DATA__ with Nike-specific shape (copy.title, prices.currentPrice, displayColors.colorDescription, colorwayImages.portraitURL)
next_data_product_wall_extractor.optional_evidence: Raw HTML string containing <script id="__NEXT_DATA__"> with productGroupings
failure_conditions: Evidence is empty string, None, or non-parseable
No __NEXT_DATA__ script tag found in HTML (for HTML input)
No 'Wall' key in __NEXT_DATA__ props
productGroupings is empty or missing
Products lack Nike-specific nesting (copy.title, prices.currentPrice)
next_data_product_wall_extractor.why_low_false_positive: Nike's product data shape is extremely site-specific. The nesting copy.title + prices.currentPrice + displayColors.colorDescription + colorwayImages.portraitURL + pdpUrl.url is unique to Nike's CMS. The productGroupings array name only appears in Nike's __NEXT_DATA__. No other common e-commerce site uses this exact structure.

step1_coerce_json.description: If evidence is str, try extract_next_data_json_from_html(evidence). If result is dict/list, continue.

step2_find_graphql_results.description: Navigate to data.props.pageProps.serverSideGqlResponseFed.productPageData.search.results OR data.serverSideGqlResponseFed.productPageData.search.results. Check for 'products' key.
step2_find_graphql_results.pass_condition: isinstance(results, dict) and isinstance(results.get('products'), list) and len(results['products']) > 0

step3_graphql_shape.description: Check first product has GraphQL cache structure: product.get('price', {}).get('listPrice', {}).get('amount') is numeric AND product.get('variants') is a non-empty list
step3_graphql_shape.code: product = results['products'][0]; price = product.get('price', {}); list_price = price.get('listPrice', {})
step3_graphql_shape.pass_condition: isinstance(list_price.get('amount'), (int, float)) and isinstance(product.get('variants'), list) and len(product['variants']) > 0

next_data_graphql_ssr_cache_extractor.required_evidence: Pre-parsed product list from GraphQL SSR cache with: title, price.listPrice.amount, variants array with mediaAssets[].assetId, seoPath
failure_conditions: Evidence is empty string, None, or non-parseable
No serverSideGqlResponseFed in __NEXT_DATA__
results.products is empty or missing
Products lack price.listPrice.amount nesting (flat price instead)
No variants array in products
next_data_graphql_ssr_cache_extractor.why_low_false_positive: GraphQL SSR cache products with price.listPrice.amount + variants[].mediaAssets[].assetId + seoPath is a distinctive Apollo/urql SSR pattern. Plain product pages never have this nested structure. The serverSideGqlResponseFed key is M&S-specific but the price.listPrice.amount shape is generic to GraphQL e-commerce implementations.

step2_parse_json_ld.description: For each script, get script.string or script.get_text(), then json.loads after html_lib.unescape. Check for @type == 'Product'
step2_parse_json_ld.code: for script in scripts: data = json.loads(html_lib.unescape(script.string)); schema_type = data.get('@type')
step2_parse_json_ld.pass_condition: At least one script contains JSON with @type == 'Product'

step3_product_has_offers.description: Check Product has offers.price or offers.lowPrice as numeric, and non-empty name
step3_product_has_offers.code: offers = product.get('offers', {}); price = offers.get('price') or offers.get('lowPrice')
step3_product_has_offers.pass_condition: isinstance(product.get('name'), str) and product['name'] != '' and (isinstance(offers.get('price'), (int, float, str)) or isinstance(offers.get('lowPrice'), (int, float, str)))

alt_step1_dict_check.description: If evidence is pre-parsed dict, check @type == 'Product' or @type == 'ItemList'

alt_step2_list_check.description: If evidence is list, check any element has @type == 'Product'
alt_step2_list_check.pass_condition: isinstance(evidence, list) and any(obj.get('@type') == 'Product' for obj in evidence if isinstance(obj, dict))

jsonld_product_extractor.required_evidence: Raw HTML string with <script type="application/ld+json"> containing @type: Product with name and offers, OR pre-parsed dict/list with Product/ItemList schema
jsonld_product_extractor.optional_evidence: Pre-parsed JSON-LD Product or ItemList object from AI network observation
failure_conditions: No <script type="application/ld+json"> in HTML
All JSON-LD scripts have @type other than Product (e.g., Organization, WebPage, BreadcrumbList)
Product exists but offers is missing or empty
JSON in script tag is malformed (JSONDecodeError)
Product name is empty string
jsonld_product_extractor.why_low_false_positive: schema.org Product markup is a standardized, machine-readable format. Sites that implement it deliberately want search engines to find product data. The @type: Product check combined with offers.price is a high-precision signal — non-product pages (home, blog, about) never contain Product schema.

step1_dict_products_key.description: Check evidence is dict with 'products' key containing a list of product dicts
step1_dict_products_key.code: isinstance(evidence, dict) and isinstance(evidence.get('products'), list) and len(evidence['products']) > 0

step2_shopify_shape.description: Check first product has Shopify-specific fields: integer 'id', string 'handle', list 'variants' with 'price' key
step2_shopify_shape.code: product = evidence['products'][0]; isinstance(product.get('id'), int) and isinstance(product.get('handle'), str) and isinstance(product.get('variants'), list)

step3_variant_price.description: Check first variant has 'price' key with numeric-like value

alt_step1_html_analytics.description: If evidence is HTML string, regex for Shopify\.analytics\.meta\.product\s*=\s*\{.*?\};
alt_step1_html_analytics.pass_condition: Match found and JSON parses to dict

alt_step2_html_products_json.description: If evidence is HTML string, regex for 'products'\s*:\s*(\[.*?\])
alt_step2_html_products_json.pass_condition: Match found and JSON parses to list with Shopify-shaped dicts

shopify_product_grid_extractor.required_evidence: Dict with 'products' key containing Shopify-shaped product objects, list of such dicts, or raw HTML with Shopify analytics
shopify_product_grid_extractor.optional_evidence: Raw HTML string containing Shopify.analytics.meta.product or products JSON in script tag
failure_conditions: Evidence is HTML string without Shopify analytics regex matches
Products lack Shopify-specific fields (id as int, handle as string, variants as list)
All prices are zero or missing
Evidence is a non-Shopify dict with 'products' key but products lack Shopify shape
shopify_product_grid_extractor.why_low_false_positive: Shopify product JSON has a distinctive shape: integer 'id', string 'handle', array 'variants' with 'compare_at_price' and 'price', array 'options' with name/values. This structure is unique to Shopify's Liquid template system. Non-Shopify stores with 'products' key use different nesting (no handle, no variants with compare_at_price).

step2_sfcc_classes.description: Check for SFCC-specific CSS classes: .product-tile__name, .product-tile__price, .pdp-link a
step2_sfcc_classes.code: has_name = bool(tile.select_one('.product-tile__name') or tile.select_one('.pdp-link a')); has_price = bool(tile.select_one('.product-tile__price .sales .value') or tile.select_one('.price .value'))
step2_sfcc_classes.pass_condition: At least one tile has both name and price elements

step3_pid_is_string.description: Check data-pid is a non-empty string (product ID)
step3_pid_is_string.code: pid = tile.get('data-pid'); isinstance(pid, str) and pid.strip() != ''
step3_pid_is_string.pass_condition: At least one tile has non-empty data-pid

alt_step1_js_fallback.description: If no DOM tiles, regex for productImpressions\s*=\s*(\[.*?\]); in HTML text
alt_step1_js_fallback.pass_condition: Match found, JSON parses to list with dicts having 'name' or 'product_name'

demandware_product_tile_extractor.required_evidence: Raw HTML string from Demandware/SFCC listing page with .product-tile[data-pid] elements or productImpressions JS
demandware_product_tile_extractor.optional_evidence: Inline JS with productImpressions array or dataLayer push with product data
failure_conditions: No .product-tile[data-pid] elements AND no productImpressions JS found
Tiles exist but all lack name/price elements (empty extraction)
data-pid is empty or not a string
JS fallback regex matches but JSON parse fails or array is empty
demandware_product_tile_extractor.why_low_false_positive: .product-tile[data-pid] is a Salesforce Commerce Cloud (Demandware) specific pattern. The data-pid attribute combined with SFCC CSS classes (product-tile__name, product-tile__price .sales .value) is unique to this platform. Generic product grids don't use data-pid. Bootstrap or Material Design grids use data-* for different purposes (data-toggle, data-dismiss).

detection_order_recommendation.reason: HTML-only, works with requests mode (no browser needed). High confidence, covers ~20% of sites. Real fixture available: superdry_com.
Shopify is ~30% of e-commerce. Dict/HTML dual input. Very distinctive shape. No external fixture yet — needs one from a real Shopify store.
HTML-only, ~30% of sites. Standardized schema.org format — high precision. No external fixture yet.
HTML-only, covers SFCC sites (big retail). DOM + JS fallback. No external fixture yet.
Requires pre-parsed evidence or browser. Covers M&S-style GraphQL SSR. Real fixture available: marksandspencer_com.
Requires pre-parsed evidence. Very Nike-specific — lowest generality. Real fixture available: nike_com.

mutual_exclusion_notes: gtm_data_attribute_extractor and demandware_product_tile_extractor both parse .product-tile but use different data attributes (data-gtm vs data-pid). Both can match on the same HTML but extract different fields. Detection should check both attributes.

next_data_product_wall_extractor and next_data_graphql_ssr_cache_extractor both consume __NEXT_DATA__ but target different product shapes. Detection must check product nesting: copy.title + prices.currentPrice = Nike wall; price.listPrice.amount + variants = GraphQL SSR.
jsonld_product_extractor can coexist with any other strategy — JSON-LD is supplementary structured data, not mutually exclusive with GTM or DOM extraction.
shopify_product_grid_extractor detects Shopify-specific fields that no other extractor checks for.

backlog_patterns_not_yet_supported.pattern: Open Graph / meta tag product data
backlog_patterns_not_yet_supported.description: og:title, og:image, product:price:amount meta tags. Common on product detail pages but not listing pages.
HTML itemscope/itemprop='name', itemprop='price', itemprop='image'. Older schema.org implementation.
Non-Next.js SPAs that hydrate product data into window state objects.
Sites where product data comes from XHR/fetch API calls (e.g., /api/products?page=1). Not embedded in HTML.
Google Analytics dataLayer product impressions (not GTM data-gtm attributes). Common on SFCC and custom sites.
